topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of stale coffee and something faintly metallic hung heavy in the air of our shared room. I walked in to find my meticulously kept journal, usually tucked beneath my pillow, lying open on my desk. The handwriting—my own, intimate, brutally honest—was spread across the page, annotated with neat, almost clinical marginalia. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake of air that felt like sandpaper in my throat. I stared, my vision blurring at the edges. When I finally managed to look up, my gaze locked onto Mark, who was lounging on his bed, a book open but his eyes fixed on me with an unnerving, placid calm. "What are you looking at?" I managed, my voice sounding brittle, like thin ice about to crack. He merely smiled, a slow, deliberate curve of his lips that didn't reach his eyes. The sheer violation of the space, the invasion of my most private thoughts, made my stomach clench into a tight, painful knot. I snatched the book, my fingers trembling so violently I almost dropped it, and backed away toward the door, needing to escape the suffocating presence of his quiet scrutiny.

[story 2]

It was the way the light caught the ink. That was what first alerted me. I was making my usual late-night cram session, the only sound the frantic scratching of my pen against the paper. When I glanced over, the page wasn't just open; it was actively being read. Mark was sitting across the room, leaning against his desk, his posture relaxed, yet intensely focused. I felt a sudden, hot flush creep up my neck, prickling my skin. I dropped my pen; it clattered loudly on the floor, a jarring sound in the sudden silence. "Hey," I said, trying to keep the tremor out of my voice, trying to sound casual. He didn't look up immediately. When he finally did, his expression was unreadable, a strange mixture of mild curiosity and something deeper, something predatory. The air seemed to thin around me. I felt a desperate urge to physically recoil, to shrink into myself, my hands tightening into fists against my thighs. The knowledge that he had absorbed the entirety of my inner landscape, cataloged it, settled over me like a cold, heavy blanket.

[story 3]

I found the evidence tucked into my desk drawer, a small, damning collection of pages. My hands shook as I pulled them out, the paper feeling strangely slick under my touch. Every entry was there: the fears I hadn't voiced aloud, the irrational anxieties, the secret hopes I kept buried deep. The reality of it hit me with a physical jolt, making my vision swim. I could feel the heat radiating off my own skin, a burning sensation spreading from my chest outward. I stood frozen by the doorway, watching Mark across the room. He was ostensibly studying, but his peripheral vision seemed to be tracking my movements, his gaze lingering on the drawer. When he finally acknowledged me, his tone was light, almost teasing, which only amplified the sheer wrongness of the situation. "Having a good night of introspection?" he asked, his tone dripping with an easy familiarity that now felt like a deliberate mockery. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my mouth making the action difficult, and I couldn't muster anything but a tight, strained silence, my entire body tense and rigid with a profound sense of exposure.
